bead.title: Build fizeau-probe-reasoning CLI for wire-form measurement
bead.description:
Build a CLI at cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning that takes a fizeau profile path (or
explicit --provider / --model / --base-url flags), sends a fixed matrix of
chat-completion requests against the live endpoint, and prints a verdict table
showing whether each reasoning knob form actually moved the reasoning token
count.

Matrix to send (one prompt — e.g. "Briefly explain what 2+2 equals."):
- reasoning: off
- reasoning: low  (KindNamed, expected PortableBudgets low=2048)
- reasoning: medium (KindNamed, expected medium=8192)
- reasoning: high (KindNamed, expected high=32768)
- reasoning: 4096 (KindTokens explicit)
- reasoning: 16384 (KindTokens explicit)

For each row capture:
- Wire body sent (so the operator can verify the translator emitted what they
  expected).
- finish_reason from the response.
- usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens (the existing extraction at
  internal/sdk/openaicompat/client.go).
- Total wall time of the request.
- A short hash of the <think> block if present (so identical thinking content
  across rows is visible — that's the flat-mapping signature).

Verdict logic per (provider, model):
- If `low`, `medium`, `high` all produce the same reasoning_tokens count
  (within ±5%): tiers are flat-mapped upstream. Recommended wire: `tokens`.
- If `4096` and `16384` produce reasoning_tokens that differ proportionally
  (within ±20% of the budget): token budgets are honored. Confirms `tokens`
  works.
- If both kinds vary meaningfully: either wire works; recommended wire defaults
  to `effort` (the more natural caller form).
- Edge: if reasoning_tokens is always 0, the upstream isn't doing thinking at
  all; recommended wire: `none` (catalog should flag the model).

Output modes:
- Default: stdout markdown table + verdict line.
- --json: machine-readable JSON for piping into catalog updaters.
- --artifact-dir <path>: write a dated artifact (default: /tmp/probe-<ts>/)
  with full request/response bodies for audit.

Run timeout: 5 minutes per request (Qwen3 thinking can take 60s+ on local
hardware).

In-scope files:
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/main.go (new)
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/probe_test.go (new) — table tests against a
  small recorded fixture (no live endpoint needed for unit tests)
- Makefile — add `make probe-reasoning` target that documents typical usage

Out-of-scope:
- Auto-writing back to the catalog (operator-controlled diff is fine in v1).
- Sweeping multiple models in one invocation (loop in shell for now).
- Telemetry / metrics emission.
bead.acceptance:
1. `go build ./cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning` succeeds.
2. `go test ./cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/...` passes (table tests verify the
   verdict logic against recorded fixtures).
3. Running `./fizeau-probe-reasoning --profile
   scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml` against the
   live OpenRouter endpoint produces a markdown table with one row per matrix
   point, a verdict line, and exit code 0.
4. The artifact directory contains the raw request and response bodies for
   each matrix point, named so the operator can inspect them without re-
   running the probe.
5. `make probe-reasoning PROFILE=<path>` runs the tool with a representative
   default matrix.

governing.title: ADR-010: Reasoning Wire Form Belongs in the Model Catalog
